methodology.description: Implement continuous scoring that actually influences discrete verdicts via reconciliation
methodology.prior_work: S417 fixed window (20->50, n<5 cap). S418a added continuous_score fields as informational only (0 verdict changes). This session (S418b) makes continuous scoring authoritative via _reconcile_verdicts().
methodology.mechanism: _reconcile_verdicts(discrete, continuous) compares cliff-edge discrete verdict with midpoint-boundary continuous verdict. When they disagree, continuous can adjust discrete by at most 1 level. Margin warnings flag scores within 0.3 of a boundary.

Increase.reason: continuous=1.84 (SUFFICIENT) adjusted discrete=1 (ADEQUATE) up by 1 level

Protect.reason: continuous=1.70 (SUFFICIENT) adjusted discrete=1 (ADEQUATE) up by 1 level

rating_changes.mechanism: avg_lp=1.84 continuous_score=1.84 > 1.5 midpoint boundary -> SUFFICIENT. Discrete threshold at 2.0 was cliff-edge; 1.84 is 92% of threshold. Reconciliation adjusts up by 1.
rating_changes.honest: Borderline case: avg_lp is genuinely below 2.0 threshold. But continuous correctly captures that 1.84 is closer to SUFFICIENT than INSUFFICIENT.

rating_changes.mechanism: proxy_k_drift=8.4% -> continuous 1.70 > 1.5 midpoint. Discrete: drift >= 6.0% = not healthy = ADEQUATE. Continuous captures that 8.4% is much closer to 6% healthy than to 20% critical.
rating_changes.honest: Margin warning active: continuous=1.70 is within 0.3 of boundary. This is the closest adjustment.

rating_changes.mechanism: Two 1->2 adjustments raised avg from 1.75 to 2.25, crossing the 2.0 SUFFICIENT threshold.

stratified.finding: DOMEX sessions (94.7% of window) produce 1.89x more L+P than non-DOMEX. Window is DOMEX-dominated so stratified comparison is underpowered.

margin_warnings: Collaborate: continuous=2.35 within 0.3 of SUFFICIENT/EXCELLENT boundary (2.5)
Protect: continuous=1.70 within 0.3 of ADEQUATE/SUFFICIENT boundary (1.5)
expect: Continuous scoring produces >=2 rating changes vs cliff-edge. DOMEX avg_lp > non-DOMEX.
actual: 3 rating changes (Increase 1->2, Protect 1->2, Overall PARTIAL->SUFFICIENT). DOMEX avg_lp (1.89) > non-DOMEX (1.0), ratio=1.89x. Both expectations confirmed.
diff: Expected >=2 changes, got exactly 3 (2 goal-level + 1 overall). Magnitude of changes larger than expected — the 2 upward adjustments cascaded into an overall verdict change from PARTIAL to SUFFICIENT. Protect adjustment is borderline (margin warning active). DOMEX comparison underpowered (n=1 non-DOMEX).
conclusion: CONFIRMED. Continuous scoring produces real verdict changes that eliminate cliff-edge artifacts. The reconciliation mechanism is conservative (max 1-level adjustment, margin warnings for borderline cases). Key risk: Protect at continuous=1.70 is the weakest adjustment — monitor for drift regression. Stratification confirms DOMEX dominance but non-DOMEX sample too small for statistical confidence.
